question: I'm approaching the end of my ECB contract and have been notified that a cooling-off period applies, which prevents me from taking up the new role I had lined up. What financial support is available during that period?
answer: Certain staff intending to engage in a new occupational activity after the end of their ECB employment must refrain, for a specified cooling-off period, from any activity that could conflict with the ECB's interests. Staff who are prohibited as a result of that cooling-off period from undertaking a specific activity and who cannot find a suitable alternative and are consequently unemployed are entitled, for the duration of the prohibition, to a special monthly allowance of 80% of the last basic salary for the first year and 60% for the year thereafter, household and child allowances, continued cover under the ECB health, long-term care and accident insurance (subject to contributions), an education allowance capped at EUR 393 per month (or EUR 764 for certified imperative educational needs), and accrual of pensionable service. (ECB Conditions of Employment, pages 3, 18–19)

question: My ECB contract ended last month and I am now receiving the unemployment allowance. I am pregnant and due in three months — does this affect the duration of my entitlement?
answer: The standard paid maternity leave for staff is 20 weeks, with four additional weeks in case of multiple births. When a former staff member in receipt of unemployment benefits gives birth during one of the entitlement periods, or is expected to give birth within six weeks of the end of such a period, that period is extended by 20 weeks, with an additional four weeks in the case of multiple births; any maternity leave taken immediately preceding the termination of the contract is deducted from this extension. (ECB Conditions of Employment, pages 13, 18)

question: I have been suspended pending an investigation and the Executive Board has decided to withhold part of my basic salary. Is there a floor below which my income cannot fall?
answer: During suspension, the part of the basic salary withheld may not exceed half, and the staff member's income may not be less than the minimum subsistence figure. That minimum subsistence figure is defined as the basic salary of a staff member in salary band A at step 1. (ECB Conditions of Employment, pages 17, 23)

question: My contract has just ended and I am receiving the ECB unemployment allowance. My child is still attending the same school outside Germany that I was claiming the education allowance for — will the ECB continue to cover the costs at the same level?
answer: The standard education allowance for primary or secondary attendance reimburses actual education costs up to EUR 393 per month, increased up to EUR 764 per month where the child attends an establishment outside Germany or a school for certified imperative educational needs. During unemployment, however, the education allowance is limited to a maximum of EUR 393 per month, except for children with certified imperative educational needs (where the cap is EUR 764), and is payable only until the end of the academic year in which the staff member becomes unemployed, provided the child remains in the same educational establishment. (ECB Conditions of Employment, pages 10, 17)

question: My spouse, who was an ECB staff member, recently passed away while in service. What benefits apply with regard to salary continuation and to our return to our country of origin?
answer: In the event of the death of a staff member, payment of their salary and allowances is extended for three months. Where termination of service is caused by death, the resettlement benefits — coverage of travel-related expenses and removal of personal belongings, together with a resettlement allowance equal to 200% of the monthly basic salary for staff entitled to a household allowance or 100% otherwise — are paid, under the conditions laid down in the Staff Rules, to the surviving spouse or recognised partner or, in their absence, to the deceased staff member's dependent children. (ECB Conditions of Employment, pages 12, 19)
